qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: BKS keystore load trusts unbounded salt length before integrity verification
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a pre-MAC allocation denial of service in `org.bouncycastle.jcajce.provider.keystore.bc.BcKeyStoreSpi.engineLoad(...)`, while the advisory targets the secret-dependent timing behavior in `org.bouncycastle.pqc.crypto.frodo.FrodoEngine.sample(...)` that was replaced with constant-time `Noise.sample(...)`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding in this session identifies the advisory's Frodo timing-channel root cause.
evidence: GHSA-p93r-85wp-75v3 describes a covert timing channel in `core/src/main/java/org/bouncycastle/pqc/crypto/frodo/FrodoEngine.java`, and the referenced fix `94abbd56413dfdac651fd878bc60253871ef5e87` removes the secret-dependent `if (t > T_chi[z])` logic from `FrodoEngine.sample(...)` and replaces it with constant-time `Noise.sample(...)`.
The session's final finding manifest at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_2ab8881e/.judge/input_manifest.json` lists exactly one final qualifying finding: `bks_keystore_pre_mac_allocation_dos`.

That finding targets unchecked `saltLength` allocation in `prov/src/main/java/org/bouncycastle/jcajce/provider/keystore/bc/BcKeyStoreSpi.java` before MAC verification, which is a different file, entrypoint, and root-cause mechanism from the Frodo timing side channel.
The run's final response records that no other candidate families were accepted for final reporting, and no final qualifying finding references Frodo, `Noise.sample`, or a timing-channel condition.
